texto: Don't judge someone for how they look or where they stand in society. That is like judging a book for its cover or where it sits on a shelf. You'll never find out whats inside unless you give a moment of your time to stop and take a look.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Georgette Tange: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Don't judge someone for how they look or where they stand in society. That is like judging a book…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, judgement, reflexao pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
